scope.included: C1 WordPress 7.0 PHP source and distribution artifacts classified to media library helpers, attachment thumbnails, image metadata/editing, uploads, MIME/filetype checks, and filesystem/upload IO by the WPHX-322 owner rules.

cross_domain_notes.reason: Attachments are posts with postmeta and taxonomy relationships. WPHX-313 fixtures must cover media semantics without taking broad post/query/storage ownership.
Theme image controls, site icons, custom headers/backgrounds, and Customizer media settings stay theme/customizer work while sharing media primitives.
REST attachment endpoints and upload routes need REST schema/transport coordination before any installed API claim.
Image, gallery, cover, file, and media-text block rendering can exercise media contracts but remain block-rendering gates.
Admin media screens, list tables, AJAX actions, and modal flows need later admin UX and list-table evidence beyond media helper behavior.
Multisite quotas/site paths and updater filesystem credential flows wrap upload/filesystem primitives but remain separate domain owners.

evidence_plan.first_haxe_candidate: A typed media/filesystem/upload adapter-contract model for upload validation, MIME/filetype decisions, attachment metadata routing, image editor selection, thumbnail generation handoff, filesystem credential routing, and hook intent.
required_next_fixtures: upload validation, overrides, unique filename, and MIME/filetype oracle fixture
attachment metadata and thumbnail generation oracle fixture
image editor selection, resize, crop, and save-path oracle fixture
media shortcode, image srcset, and gallery output fixture
filesystem credentials, direct/FTP/SSH2 backend routing fixture
multisite upload quota and path handoff fixture
evidence_plan.claim: This surface manifest bounds WPHX-313 and names fixture gates. It does not claim public PHP replacement, Haxe-owned runtime logic, live filesystem/image-library parity, REST/admin upload parity, or installed-distribution media parity.
